item.title: Trump threatens to restrict stadium deal with Washington Commanders if they don't change name back to 'Redskin
item.description: President Donald Trump on Sunday escalated his call for the Washington Commanders to change their name back to the "Redskins," threatening to restrict the NFL team's stadium deal if they don't, though it's unclear how he would be able to.

item.title: Donald Trump pays tribute to 'great friend' Hulk Hogan
item.description: T﻿he controversial wrestling icon backed Trump in the latter's 2024 presidential campaign.

item.title: Fury over plans to lock public out of Albert Park for three weeks before F1 grand prix
item.description: Melbourne community sporting groups have slammed new plans to lock them out of Albert Park for even longer during the Formula 1 grand prix.

item.title: US university student named as snowboarder who died at Perisher Ski Resort
item.description: The University of Utah student had been in the Snowy Mountains since May, working for a company offering outdoor expeditions and activities to young people.

item.title: 'Heartbreaking': The hidden toll of Sydney's wettest August in 27 years
item.description: The seemingly endless deluge came at a high price for young people – weekend sports.

item.title: Four charged in Brownlow Medal betting probe
item.description: T﻿he four Melbourne men are accused of corrupt conduct and cheating at gambling on the AFL's top prize.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce engaged
item.description: "Your English teacher and your gym teacher are getting married," Swift, 35, captioned the carousel of five photographs showing the moment Kelce, 35, got down on one knee.

item.title: Dementia now the leading cause of death for Australians
item.description: Without intervention, one million people could be living with the condition within a decade, a new report warns.

item.title: 'We're going to save lives': Routine breast cancer test could be used to screen heart disease
item.description: It's the routine test taken by millions of Australian women to detect breast cancer, but there are new details of how mammograms and artificial intelligence could be used to detect heart disease.

item.title: Company fined $118k for alleged illegal medicinal cannabis advertising
item.description: Dispensed Pty Ltd was hit with six infringement notices for advertising prescription-only medicinal cannabis on third-party websites and social media.

item.title: The new MRI machine in major Sydney hospital that's freezing tumours and saving patients from debilitating pai
item.description: Patients can be in and out of hospital in a day and have a quicker recovery time than would be expected from normal, more invasive surgery.

item.title: Measles case detected in Brisbane as Queensland outbreak continues to grow
item.description: The case is believed to have been imported from overseas, with a return traveller from Bali visiting multiple locations in Brisbane and the Sunshine Coast.

item.title: Exposure sites revealed after measles case confirmed in Sydney
item.description: The person was revealed to be someone who was in contact with a recently reported case in northern Sydney, and was infectious when visiting a number of locations in Dee Why and Manly in late September.

item.title: Last drinks? We could be on the cusp of a great Australian cultural change
item.description: A study of more than 23,000 Aussies found abstaining from alcohol is on the rise, as is weekly alcohol consumption.

item.title: Females carry a higher genetic risk of depression than males, scientists say
item.description: A﻿ new study shows female DNA has twice the warning signs of the condition compared with men.

item.title: Medical bodies sound alarm over 'unregulated' medicinal cannabis industry in letter to Health Minister Mark Bu
item.description: The Australian Medical Association (AMA) and the Pharmacy Guild of Australia believe the prescription of the drug is being exploited.

item.title: Doctors sound the alarm as flu rates spike, vaccination rates drop around Australia
item.description: There have been over 410,000 lab-confirmed cases of influenza in 2025 according to the Royal Australian College of GPs.

item.title: Contraceptives to be two-thirds cheaper for hundreds of thousands of Aussie women after new pills, device adde
item.description: S﻿ome women could save up to 88 per cent on one contraceptive device, with costs being capped at $25 from next year.

item.title: Australia spent $19.7 billion fighting this one condition as health spending rises
item.description: The government alone spent $188.2 billion on health last year.

item.title: Man dies at Sydney hospital after gas allegedly sabotaged, woman proclaims innocence as investigations continu
item.description: Police are investigating if a patient would have died had a woman not allegedly tampered with the gas.

item.title: Experts bust common food myths, including one that half of all Aussies believe
item.description: It is one of many myths the Food Information Safety Council is looking to bust.

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines carry substantially less risk to kids' heart health than infection, new study finds
item.description: Children who catch COVID-19 are at risk of contracting dangerous heart inflammation, but don't face the same danger from getting vaccinated against the virus.

item.title: Experimental daily pill may lower cholesterol in patients who don't see enough benefit with statins alone
item.description: It is similar to other pills available in Australia already.

item.title: 'Never wash raw chicken': More than half of Australians risking higher chance of food poisoning with common ha
item.description: P﻿eople think this measure is protecting them, but experts warn it could do the opposite.

item.title: Brisbane and Gold Coast residents warned after measles cases detected at concert, tourist attractions
item.description: Residents of two major cities are being told to be on high alert.

item.title: Cost of treatment for deadly cancer slashed dramatically after PBS addition
item.description: Those with multiple myeloma, an incurable blood cancer, would previously pay up to $150,000 for treatment.

item.title: Parents of teens tragically killed by methanol poisoning team up with government for schoolies abroad warning
item.description: The mothers of two Melbourne teenagers who tragically died last year have teamed up with the government in a warning campaign for young travellers.

item.title: New mRNA flu vaccine far more effective than existing jabs, but has some limitations, study finds
item.description: A newly developed mRNA-based flu vaccine is almost 35 per cent more effective against the most common form of influenza, according to a new study.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Australians aren't eating enough burritos. It just cost one company hundreds of millions of dollars
item.description: I﻿ts share price has dropped by more than half in the last 12 months.

item.title: Employees at Aussie start-up Eucalyptus set to receive windfall after $1.6 billion acquisition
item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: WiseTech Global set to cut up to 2000 jobs for 'AI efficiency'
item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.title: Domino's Pizza sheds 10 per cent of customers after reducing discounts
item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.title: Man fired after pouring cooking oil down the drain outside popular bakery
item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Company behind Australian cultural icon Hills Hoist could soon be sold
item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.title: Almost 40,000 Budget Direct customers allegedly overcharged $3.3 million
item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: VB pins hopes on mid-strength as Aussies drop classic beers
item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff
item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: Aussie software giant Atlassian cuts 1600 jobs in push to AI
item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.title: Australia's No.1 retailer named as last year's winner plunges
item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.title: At least 23,000 jobs in tech have been lost to AI this year. Experts say worse is still to come
item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: Atlassian accused of illegally firing employee who suggested boss was 'rich jerk'
item.description: P﻿rosecutors argued that the employee was merely acting in the company's "Open Company, No Bullshit" philosophy.

item.title: Iconic Stonewall Hotel goes under after 28 years as administrators appointed
item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: 'I don't accept it': Kyle Sandilands reveals contract terminated by ARN
item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.title: How does Australia's emergency fuel stockpile compare to the rest of the world?
item.description: The figures don't paint an overly pretty picture for Australia – although the government says there's a good reason for it.

item.title: Businesses urge government to decrease $3 billion tax to avoid 'even worse outcomes' in fuel crisis
item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: What is the 'sovereign citizen' movement?
item.description: It's the belief system of the alleged Porepunkah cop killer, but it has a long and disturbing history.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.title: 'Doesn't pass the pub test': Hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges
item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.title: 'It has been a privilege': Cusack's Furniture to close late 2026
item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: As fuel excise set to be halved, when will fuel prices in Australia go down?
item.description: With key taxes being slashed, when will Australians finally see relief at the pump?

item.title: Western Australia enacts emergency fuel powers as PM urges calm
item.description: The state's six fuel suppliers will have 48 hours to hand over "sensitive information", or risk being fined up to $100,000 per day.

item.title: PM defends lacklustre national address
item.description: The speech is widely rumoured to involve financial assistance for businesses hit by soaring fuel costs.

item.title: What our readers thought of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's address to the nation
item.description: "A waste of time" – those were the words nine.com.au readers used over and over again.

item.title: Neo-Nazi forced offline after alleged threats to MPs
item.description: Joel Davis, 31, has been in custody on remand since his arrest in November for allegedly calling for the "rhetorical rape" of independent Wentworth MP Allegra Spender.

item.title: We were all set for prime time, then we were let down with a thud by our leaders
item.description: Opinion: In terms of doom and gloom, we're being bombarded at the moment with bad news.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.title: Shocking report blows the lid on where government funding is being spent
item.description: What's shocking is that 68 per cent of operators are unlicensed and one of those organisations received nearly $34 million in funding last financial year.

item.title: Spending cuts, tax reforms flagged in next month's federal budget
item.description: More and more Australians are draining﻿ their savings just to stay afloat as hopes for cost-of-living relief in next month's federal budget slowly fade.

item.title: Shopper caught trying to defraud Aussie small business using AI photos of 'damaged' order
item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.title: Experts send dire fuel crisis warning as PM travels to Singapore to lock in supplies
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is travelling to Singapore in a bid to shore up Australia's fuel supply, trading on the country's reputation as a food bowl.

item.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after UK bars rapper Ye over antisemitic remarks
item.description: The rapper has drawn widespread condemnation for making antisemitic remarks and voicing admiration for Adolf Hitler.

item.title: VIktor Orban offers to be 'mouse' to Vladimir Putin's 'lion' in reportedly leaked phone call
item.description: V﻿iktor Orban offered to be the "mouse" to the Russian president's "lion", the reported transcripts read.

item.title: Nationals leader Matt Canavan unveils plan to create an 'Australia on steroids'
item.description: Matt Canavan has called for new cities, more babies and a domestic space industry.

item.title: Motorcycle rider allegedly clocked at 300km/h during police pursuit
item.description: Officers first detected a motorcycle speeding along the Princes Freeway﻿ in Lara around 10pm yesterday.

item.title: 'Liquid gold': Queensland pushes to fast-track expansion of outback oil field in response to fuel crisis
item.description: Australia produces very little oil itself, getting most of it from Asian neighbours, but David Crisafulli wants that to change.

item.title: Sydney tech giant Canva snaps up two local AI companies as $60 billion company teases 'even bigger' announceme
item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.title: Australian airlines among 'most exposed' if jet fuel runs dry
item.description: T﻿heir bottom line will be impacted by any prolonged disruption of aviation fuel, analysts warn.

item.description: The US president described the brutal attack as "﻿one of the most vicious things you will ever see" as he shared CCTV of the incident on Truth Social.

item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.description: Alarm bells went off in small business owner Tanielle's mind as soon as she saw it.

item.description: Payslip complacency has reached remarkable highs.

item.title: Three senior Victorian Labor MPs announce resignation from politics
item.description: The resignations have triggered a cabinet reshuffle months ahead of Victoria's state election.

item.title: State political donation laws struck out by High Court
item.description: A state's political donation and campaign funding laws have been ruled constitutionally invalid, in a case that could have national implications.

item.title: Virgin to cut flights, raise costs after $40 million fuel hit
item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.title: Australia signs key deal with Brunei amid fuel crisis, recession fears
item.description: Australia has signed a deal with Asian neighbour Brunei that will get more fertiliser to farmers at a time where they continue to be smashed by the fuel crisis.

item.description: "﻿The consequences of this conflict on the other side of the world are already very serious for Australians."

item.description: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries will build the first three Mogami-class frigates in Japan. Australia plans to b...

item.title: Big hint Apple's first foldable smartphone is coming this year
item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.title: Gas tax would make Australia $17 billion and cut domestic prices, Senate told
item.description: Norway and Qatar have done it. Now there's a growing push for Australia to make the move that "solves all those problems".

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after 15 years
item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.title: 'Should never have happened': Minister admits failings after two foster children allowed to live with serial k
item.description: "It was closed based on unverified information about Arthurell and his possible incapacity," the minister admitted.

item.title: 'We've been sold out': Influencer's impassioned plea to Senate
item.description: K﻿onrad Benjamin, a former teacher who founded Punter's Politics, let loose before a Senate committee yesterday.

item.title: 'ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks': Government flags crackdown, cost-cutting in NDIS
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler delivered the sweeping changes in a National Press Club Address and said the NDIS will no longer ﻿"be an ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks".

item.title: Over 200 million litres of extra diesel coming to Australia
item.description: P﻿rime Minister Anthony Albanese said Australian buyers had secured four extra cargo loads.

item.title: How a crummy cake kicked off a food-delivery crisis in China
item.description: T﻿he inquiry has been marked by scuffles between investigators and delivery service employees, a feigned medical emergency and hastily scribbled notes to "stay silent".

item.description: They seem harmless upon first glance, but look a little closer and you'll see the truth.

item.description: "That flag represents our people, our freedoms, and every Australian who has stood under it to serve."

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $113.5 billion takeover of the Hollywood giant
item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.title: Adelaide family forced to crowdfund boy's life-saving cancer therapy after unfulfilled promise
item.description: An Adelaide family has been forced to crowdfund to send their child abroad for life-saving cancer therapy after an unfulfilled promise to bring the treatment to South Australia.

item.title: US millionaire trophy hunter killed by herd of elephants
item.description: A US millionaire and enthusiastic big game hunter has died by being trampled to death by elephants in Africa.

item.title: Today in History - April 26: Three volunteers stepped up for task they were told would kill them
item.description: A safety test mistake at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant in modern-day Ukraine caused the worst nuclear accident in history on April 26, 1986.

item.title: US says it's hunting for explosive mines in latest push to open the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Sweeping for underwater explosives could take months, experts say.

item.title: While other retailers close up shop, business is booming for these once-taboo stores
item.description: People used to shame it, but clearly something is working for Aussie shoppers.

item.description: US president Donald Trump has been rushed to safety with wife Melania after shots were fired at an exclusive dinner in Washington DC.

item.description: H﻿e was the only US president not to attend a White House Correspondents' Association dinner - until this weekend.

item.description: Iran has offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the US lifting its blockade on the country and an end to the war.

item.description: Anyone who has been to these locations recently should monitor for symptoms, authorities say.

item.description: The five-year-old was last seen when she was put to bed late on Saturday.

item.title: Gunman, reportedly age 89, opens fire at two locations in Greek capital, wounding several people
item.description: A police operation was underway to locate the gunman, who Greek media reported was an 89-year-old man.

item.title: United Arab Emirates says it will leave OPEC effective May 1
item.description: The oil cartel will be stripped of one of its largest producers and further weaken its leverage over global oil supplies and prices.

item.title: Robot dogs with Musk and Zuckerberg heads roam around Berlin gallery in Beeple's new exhibit
item.description: Robot dogs with hyper-realistic silicone heads modelled after world-renowned figures – including Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos – can be seen roaming around a Berlin gallery.

item.title: Man pleads guilty to plotting attack on a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
item.description: The Vienna plot drew comparisons to a 2017 attack by a suicide bomber at an Ariana Grande concert in Manchester, England, that killed 22 people.

item.title: Trump thrills in apparent royal ties as he welcomes King Charles to the White House on historic day
item.description: US President Donald Trump has joked about his aspirations to live at Buckingham Palace as King Charles and Queen Camilla kick off the second day of their US state visit.

item.title: Today in History - April 29: Doctor trapped in Antarctic base cuts out his own appendix
item.description: Thousands of kilometres from help, a Russian doctor had to cut out his own appendix in Antarctica after falling ill on April 29, 1961.

item.title: Ex-FBI chief James Comey indicted over seashell photo that officials say threatened Trump
item.description: T﻿he post seems innocuous enough, but it could land the Trump critic in prison for 10 years.

item.title: Donald Trump's face, golden signature to be included on some US passports
item.description: Donald Trump's face will be placed on American passports, the US State Department has announced.

item.title: Trump reveals his late mother's royal crush during White House address
item.description: An awkward aside cut through a formal White House address as Donald Trump told King Charles III his mother once had a crush on him.

item.description: "This particular phone call was was very harsh. I just hung up the phone."

item.title: Giant golden statue of Trump installed at his Florida golf course
item.description: A nearly seven-metre tall golden statue of Donald Trump has been installed at one of his golf courses.

item.title: 'Yes, it's sexy': Pauline Hanson gifted $2.1 million new plane by Gina Rinehart
item.description: The One Nation Leader also received $2 million in donations.

item.description: T﻿he trio voiced protests about a proposed expansion of a major oil poject in WA.

item.title: PM dances around tax changes as opposition quashes support

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: 'Violence will never succeed': Charles' nod to shooting in historic speech

item.title: Man accused of killing Labor strategist to fight charge
item.description: A young man accused of the one-punch killing of political strategist Tim Picton near a nightclub in the early hours has pleaded not guilty to manslaughter.

item.title: Man charged with trying to kill Trump at dinner took photo with knife in hotel, investigators say
item.description: The man charged with trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner took a picture of himself minutes earlier, outfitted with an ammunition bag, a shoulder gun holster and a sheathed knife.

item.title: Today in History - April 30: If militants were watching another channel, they'd know they were about to die
item.description: Separatists seized the Iranian embassy in London on April 30, 1980, taking 26 people hostage.

item.title: Police release bodycam footage of suspected London Jewish terror attackers
item.description: Two Jewish people were stabbed in north London, in what police have called an act of terrorism.

item.title: Woolworths sales surge but supermarket cuts profit forecast amid petrol price spike
item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.title: How death threat charge against James Comey could put Donald Trump and his allies in hot water
item.description: The Trump administration will soon be asking a judge and jury to believe that four digits constitute a death threat.

item.title: Aussie meth use surges, ranks second globally after doubling in a decade
item.description: Wastewater data confirms a massive geographical divide.

item.title: Men accused of flying drone over Wollongong to traffic drugs
item.description: P﻿olice arrested them when the device crashed into an apartment complex.

item.title: Olympic breakdancer Raygun speaks after losing university job
item.description: The breakdancer who found fame at the Paris Olympics is embarking on a new chapter.

item.title: Former federal Labor leader Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k for offensive tweet to MP
item.description: T﻿he former federal Labor leader will be forced to pay Alex Greenwich $100,000 after a tweet directed at him was found to be unlawful sexual harassment.

item.title: Famous voice actor's excitement ahead of anticipated game launch
item.description: Troy Baker has an idea locked-and-loaded for another Indian Jones video game.

item.title: Christchurch shooter to remain behind bars for life after court rejects bid to overturn conviction
item.description: The panel of three judges dismissed Brenton Tarrant’s claim that harsh prison conditions prompted his involuntarily admission to the terrorism, murder and attempted murder charges he faced.

item.title: Search continues for Sharon: A timeline of events
item.description: The five year old has been missing since Saturday night, with police launching one of their largest operations in decades to find her.

item.title: Devastating invasive species discovered in Queensland neighbourhood
item.description: Investigators have destroyed the nest, but warn that non-compliance with biosecurity measures could result in a $500,000 fine.

item.title: Commonwealth Bank forecasts anticipated changes to property tax benefits could fall flat
item.description: The capital gains tax discount and negative gearing are widely blamed for contributing to the housing crisis.

item.title: 'Double whammy' leaves 4.4 million Aussies paying up to $815 more a year
item.description: A proposed private health insurance rebate cut and increased premiums are hitting the country's most vulnerable.

item.title: 'Unimaginable trauma': Parents pay tribute to two kids killed in NSW house fire
item.description: The family of the two children who died in a house fire in NSW's Hawkesbury region have released an emotional statement about their "unimaginable tragedy".

item.title: Trump obsession reaches new heights
item.description: The hypersonic missile is yet to be declared "fit for use" - and cost a cool $41 million each.

item.title: First report into antisemitism royal commission recommends national gun buyback scheme, more counter-terrorism
item.description: T﻿he first report from a royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion in Australia has been published today, less than five months after 15 innocent people were gunned down the Bondi Beach terror attack.

item.title: Syria says Australia won't repatriate families from camp for those with alleged ties to ISIS
item.description: T﻿he Australian government refused to repatriate women and children who had secured passports and a flight out of Syria.

item.title: Rebel Wilson styles herself as 'truth-teller' regarding sexual harassment claim amid film feud
item.description: Wilson has denied having any involvement in ordering or authoring the websites that described the producer as the "Indian Ghislaine Maxwell" and a sex trafficker.

item.title: This country just overtook England as Australia's top overseas country of birth
item.description: New statistics have shown that Australia's number one overseas country of origin has been surpassed for the first time ever.

item.title: Police investigate whether brothel shooting linked to recent attacks on Melbourne hospitality venues
item.description: A﻿ task force has been created to investigate dozens of firebombings or shootings at the city's venues in recent months.

item.title: Two dead, 10 injured after light plane crashes through hangar roof in South Australia
item.description: Thick black smoke drifted over the tarmac after the ﻿DA42 twin-engine aircraft crashed into the flight school hangar shortly after take-off and burst into flames.

item.title: Queensland Police search unit owned by prominent surgeon, the partner of ex-premier Annastacia Palaszczuk
item.description: Police said they are collecting the evidence as a part of an ongoing investigation.

item.title: Jacinta Price reveals she's aunt of NT girl believed to have been abducted
item.description: T﻿he search for the missing five-year-old girl has entered day five amid growing concerns for her safety.

item.title: Huge gap being left in wills, as loved ones navigating challenges of digital life after death
item.description: Most Australians plan for what happens to their money, their home, and their belongings after they die - but are forgetting a huge asset.

item.title: Trump celebrates Supreme Court decision that could sway elections for years to come
item.description: US civil rights leaders are decrying a Supreme Court decision that could cost countless non-white politicians their seats in Congress.

item.title: Today in History - May 1: Why CIA put spy pilot's wife in a mental institution
item.description: US spy pilot Gary Powers was charged with espionage after his plane was shot down over Russia on May 1, 1960.

item.title: The 'archaic' rule forcing Australians with a disability to choose between 'love and survival'
item.description: Abby Brothers has been with her partner for 15 years - but if they move in together, she'll lose $1100 a fortnight.

item.title: Man charged after paramedic stabbed while stopping for coffee
item.description: The male paramedic was outside a cafe on Broadway in Reservoir around 11am when a man got out of a small, red car and stabbed him in the upper body, police allege.

item.title: 'Grieving and remembering': Family's message after five-year-old murdered
item.description: The family of Kumanjayi Little Baby has called for calm across Alice Springs to allow them to grieve and conduct sorry business.

item.title: Motorists in Victoria offered $186 rego rebate to cushion cost-of-living crisis
item.description: Car registration in Victoria will be slashed by 20 per cent from early next month until July 31.

item.title: Purported Jeffrey Epstein suicide note being kept hidden by court: report
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein's former cellmate claims the paedophile financier wrote a suicide note, which has been kept secret by a court, the New York Times claims.

item.title: Multi-agency search underway for Australian missing on Canada hiking trail
item.description: The 62-year-old has not been heard from in two weeks.

item.title: US elections suspended so government can redraw the district lines
item.description: The US state of Louisiana has suspended voting in an election due to begin tomorrow in order to redraw districts to elect another Republican member of Congress.

item.title: Australian travellers warned, UK Prime Minister heckled at terror attack site as Britain raises terror level
item.description: The UK has upgraded its national terrorism threat level﻿ from "substantial" to "severe" the day after the antisemitic stabbing attack in London.

item.title: Deadly disease not seen in Australia for nearly 20 years detected in wastewater in Perth
item.description: The deadly virus had been thought eradicated.

item.title: Ex-Liberal MP continues fight against child sex charges
item.description: Rory Amon has consistently argued he thought a 13-year-old boy was of legal age before meeting him at the youth's apartment block in 2017.

item.title: LIVE: War is changing what groceries Australians are buying, says supermarket giant
item.description: Donald Trump says he will remove tariffs on Scottish whiskey ﻿"in honour" of King Charles.

item.title: Producer reveals 'tricky' position in Rebel's film feud as fiery trial draws to a close
item.description: "Rebel Wilson is my boss, as is Amanda Ghost. I'm trying to be respectful to both parties".

item.title: Primary school teacher accused of filming inside toilet
item.description: A magistrate says there are fears an arrested teacher could destroy evidence after police were allegedly given false passwords.

item.title: 'National disgrace': Jacinta Price reacts to death of five-year-old niece
item.description: T﻿he suspected murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby sparks debate among the country's top leaders.

item.title: 'Cannot recall': Indigenous commissioner's telling admission
item.description: H﻿er comments lay bare the history between police and community members of Alice Springs.

item.title: 'A soft target for crooks': Shocking extent of NDIS fraud revealed
item.description: "If you think it is acceptable to prey on people with disabilities, you do not belong in the NDIS. You belong in prison, and we are coming for you."

item.title: Surveillance footage shows gunman charging through officers at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: New surveillance camera footage has been released showing the White House Correspondents' Dinner gunman charging through security.

item.title: 'Loophole' to close as alleged paramedic stabber in court
item.description: The Mobile Intensive Care Ambulance paramedic was allegedly stabbed in the neck and face in an unprovoked a...

item.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with three counts of rape
item.description: The prominent surgeon allegedly assaulted a woman in her 30s at a Gold Coast apartment.

item.title: Two critical, eight more injured after car driven by 14-year-old boy triggers four-car smash, police say
item.description: Two people are in a critical condition and eight others are injured after a car allegedly driven by a 14-year-old boy triggered a four-car smash near Hervey Bay on Queensland's Fraser Coast.

item.title: 'No place in hell' for father over fatal crash cover-up
item.description: The mother of two children killed in a high-speed crash has said an attempted cover-up by the driver's father was driven by cowardice and a lack of humanity.

item.title: May begins with storms, gusty winds and cooler change for southern states
item.description: It follows a stint of unseasonably warm weather.

item.title: Rotting whale towed from beach as shark warning issued along NSW coast
item.description: Beachgoers have already been forced to evacuate following sightings.

item.title: Family of Kumanjayi Little Baby calls for calm to hold in Alice Springs
item.description: The heartbroken community awaits charges against a suspected killer a night after intense unrest.
